title: Find all ERROR lines
task: Display every line containing `ERROR` in the application log.

title: Case-insensitive INFO search
task: Display lines containing `info`, regardless of letter case.

title: Show line numbers
task: Find `WARN` lines and include their line numbers.

title: Match several log levels
task: Display lines containing either `ERROR` or `FATAL`.

title: Count failed logins
task: Count lines containing `Failed password` in the authentication log.

title: Exclude INFO lines
task: Display application-log lines that do not contain `INFO`.

title: Match a whole word
task: Find occurrences of the whole word `root` in the authentication log.

title: Match a line prefix
task: Display access-log requests coming from IP address `192.168.1.10`.

title: Find HTTP 4xx responses
task: Display access-log lines whose response status is in the 400–499 range.

title: Find GET or POST requests
task: Display requests whose HTTP method is either GET or POST.

title: Search recursively for TODO or FIXME
task: Search the entire `projects` tree for unfinished-work markers.

title: List files containing ERROR
task: Print only the names of log files that contain `ERROR`.

title: List files without ERROR
task: Print names of log files that contain no `ERROR` line.

title: Match an entire line
task: Find lines in `words.txt` that are exactly lowercase `apple`.

title: Count apple ignoring case
task: Count lines equal to or containing `apple` regardless of case.

title: Names starting with A or R
task: Display names beginning with uppercase A or R.

title: Engineering employees
task: Display employee records belonging to Engineering.

title: Inactive employees
task: Display employee records whose final CSV field is `inactive`.

title: Show context around FATAL
task: Display the FATAL line together with one line before and one line after it.

title: Extract only IP addresses
task: Extract every IPv4-like address from the access log.

title: Count requests per IP
task: Create a frequency table showing how many requests each IP sent.

title: Extract key=value pairs
task: Extract all `name=value` fragments from the application log.

title: Locate blank lines
task: Show the line numbers of blank lines in `multiline.txt`.

title: Remove blank lines
task: Display only nonblank lines from `multiline.txt`.

title: Search selected file extensions
task: Search only Markdown files for TODO or FIXME markers.

title: Exclude an archive directory
task: Search Python files for `print` but skip `projects/archive`.

title: Use a fixed-string search
task: Search literally for `division by zero` in the project tree.

title: Match an exact configuration line
task: Find a line that is exactly `DEBUG=false`.

title: Find server-error requests
task: Display web requests that returned an HTTP 5xx status.

title: Extract error and fatal messages
task: Print only the message text after `ERROR` or `FATAL` in `app.log`.

title: Print selected CSV fields
task: Print each employee name followed by the department.

title: Use a custom output separator
task: Print employee name, role, and city separated by ` | `.

title: Filter high salaries
task: Print names and salaries of employees earning more than 80,000.

title: Combine multiple conditions
task: Find active Engineering employees located in Kolkata.

title: Count employees by status
task: Count active and inactive employees.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR>1 {count[$8]++} END{for (s in count) print s, count[s]}' data/employees.csv | sort

title: Calculate total payroll
task: Calculate the sum of all employee salaries.

title: Calculate average salary
task: Calculate the average employee salary.

title: Find the minimum salary
task: Print the employee with the lowest salary.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR==2 {min=$5; name=$2} NR>2 && $5<min {min=$5; name=$2} END{print name,min}' data/employees.csv

title: Find the maximum salary
task: Print the employee with the highest salary.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR==2 {max=$5; name=$2} NR>2 && $5>max {max=$5; name=$2} END{print name,max}' data/employees.csv

title: Count employees per department
task: Print a department-wise employee count.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR>1 {count[$3]++} END{for (d in count) print d,count[d]}' data/employees.csv | sort

title: Average salary per department
task: Calculate average salary for each department.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR>1 {sum[$3]+=$5; count[$3]++} END{for (d in sum) printf "%s %.2f\n",d,sum[d]/count[d]}' data/employees.csv | sort

title: Payroll by city
task: Calculate total salary paid in each city.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR>1 {pay[$6]+=$5} END{for (c in pay) print c,pay[c]}' data/employees.csv | sort

title: Calculate each sale amount
task: Print sale ID, product, and `quantity × unit_price`.

title: Calculate total sales revenue
task: Calculate revenue across every sales record.

title: Revenue by region
task: Calculate total sales revenue for each region.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR>1 {rev[$3]+=$6*$7} END{for (r in rev) print r,rev[r]}' data/sales.csv | sort

title: Highest-value sale
task: Print the sale ID and value of the largest single sales transaction.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR>1 {v=$6*$7; if(v>max){max=v; id=$1; product=$5}} END{print id,product,max}' data/sales.csv

title: Quantity sold by product
task: Calculate the total quantity sold for every product.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR>1 {qty[$5]+=$6} END{for (p in qty) print p,qty[p]}' data/sales.csv | sort

title: Student average marks
task: Print each student and the average of math, science, and English.

title: Assign pass or fail
task: Mark a student PASS when the three-subject average is at least 60.

title: Find the top student
task: Print the student with the highest three-subject average.

title: Count students by major
task: Print how many students belong to each major.
cmd: awk -F, 'NR>1 {count[$3]++} END{for (m in count) print m,count[m]}' data/students.csv | sort

title: Find out-of-stock items
task: Print inventory items whose stock is zero.

title: Inventory value per item
task: Print each item and its stock value (`stock × price`).

title: Inventory value by category
task: Calculate total inventory value for each category.
cmd: awk -F '\t' 'NR>1 {value[$3]+=$4*$5} END{for (c in value) print c,value[c]}' data/inventory.tsv | sort

title: Users with Bash shells
task: Print usernames whose login shell is `/bin/bash`.

title: Format username and home directory
task: Print `username -> home_directory` for every user.

title: Count HTTP status codes
task: Create a count of each response status in the access log.
cmd: awk '{count[$9]++} END{for (s in count) print s,count[s]}' data/web_access.log | sort -n

title: Sum bytes by HTTP status
task: Calculate total response bytes sent for every HTTP status.
cmd: awk '{bytes[$9]+=$10} END{for (s in bytes) print s,bytes[s]}' data/web_access.log | sort -n

title: Find the most active IP
task: Print the IP address that made the most requests.
cmd: awk '{count[$1]++} END{for (ip in count) if(count[ip]>max){max=count[ip]; top=ip} print top,max}' data/web_access.log

title: Count application log levels
task: Count INFO, DEBUG, WARN, ERROR, and FATAL entries.
cmd: awk '{count[$3]++} END{for (level in count) print level,count[level]}' data/app.log | sort

title: List every regular file
task: List all regular files under the lab directory.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f | sort

title: List every directory
task: List all directories under `projects`.

title: Find lowercase .log files
task: Find files whose names end in lowercase `.log`.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f -name '*.log' | sort

title: Case-insensitive text files
task: Find `.txt` files regardless of extension case.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f -iname '*.txt' | sort

title: Find names containing spaces
task: Find filesystem entries whose names contain a space.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -name '* *' | sort

title: Find empty files
task: List all zero-byte regular files.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f -empty | sort

title: Find empty directories
task: List directories containing no entries.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type d -empty | sort

title: Find files larger than 5 KiB
task: List regular files larger than 5 KiB.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f -size +5k | sort

title: Find files from 1 to 5 KiB
task: List files at least roughly 1 KiB but no larger than 5 KiB.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f -size +1k -size -5k | sort

title: Find executable files
task: List regular files executable by at least one user class.

title: Find files with exact mode 600
task: Find regular files whose permission bits are exactly `600`.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f -perm 600 | sort

title: Find files older than one year
task: Find archived files modified more than 365 days ago.

title: Find files newer than a reference
task: Find files in the January log directory newer than `server1.log`.

title: Find files older than a reference
task: Find log files older than January’s `server1.log`.

title: Limit search depth
task: List regular files no deeper than two levels below the current directory.

title: Require a minimum depth
task: List project files located at depth 3 or deeper.

title: Prune an archive directory
task: Find Python files under `projects` without descending into `projects/archive`.

title: Match multiple extensions
task: Find regular files ending in `.py` or `.sh`.

title: Find files owned by the current user
task: List regular files owned by your current username.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f -user "$(id -un)" | sort

title: Find symbolic links
task: List all symbolic links, whether valid or broken.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type l | sort

title: Find broken symbolic links
task: List symbolic links whose targets do not exist.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -xtype l | sort

title: Run wc on found files
task: Count lines in every `.log` file under `logs`.

title: Run grep on found files
task: Find TODO or FIXME markers in project Markdown files.

title: Print size and path
task: Print each backup file’s byte size followed by its path.

title: Rank files by size
task: Display the five largest regular files in the lab.
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f -printf '%s %p\n' | sort -nr | head -n 5

title: Find the newest file
task: Print the most recently modified regular file.

title: Print only basenames
task: Print only the basenames of Python files.

title: Safely find leading-dash names
task: Find files whose basename begins with `-`.

title: Find files not ending in .log
task: List regular files under `logs` whose names do not end in `.log`.

title: Find world-writable files
task: List regular files writable by “others.”
cmd: find data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports -type f -perm -002 -print | sort

title: Count lines, words, and bytes
task: Show line, word, and byte counts for `employees.csv`.

title: Preview the beginning of a file
task: Display the header and first three employee records.

title: Preview the end of a file
task: Display the final five sales records.

title: Extract CSV columns with cut
task: Print employee name and department columns.

title: Extract passwd-style fields
task: Print username and shell from `users.txt`.

title: Sort words alphabetically
task: Sort `words.txt` using bytewise ordering.

title: Create a case-insensitive unique list
task: Print unique words while treating uppercase and lowercase forms as equal.

title: Count word frequencies
task: Count each word ignoring case and rank by frequency.

title: Numerically sort salaries
task: Display the five highest-paid employee records.
cmd: { head -n 1 data/employees.csv; tail -n +2 data/employees.csv | sort -t, -k5,5nr | head -n 5; }

title: Sort by two keys
task: Sort employees by department, then by descending salary.

title: Convert text to uppercase
task: Convert every name to uppercase.

title: Squeeze repeated spaces
task: Collapse repeated spaces in the application log to one space.

title: Combine files side by side
task: Pair each name with a department using a tab separator.

title: Join related tables
task: Attach department codes to employee rows.
cmd: join -t, -1 3 -2 1 <(tail -n +2 data/employees.csv | sort -t, -k3,3) <(tail -n +2 data/dept_codes.csv | sort -t, -k1,1) | head

title: Compare two sorted sets
task: Show names that are employees but never appear as salespeople.
cmd: comm -23 <(tail -n +2 data/employees.csv | cut -d, -f2 | sort -u) <(tail -n +2 data/sales.csv | cut -d, -f4 | sort -u)

title: Replace text with sed
task: Display `app.log` with `INFO` changed to `INFORMATION`.

title: Delete blank lines with sed
task: Display `multiline.txt` without blank lines.

title: Print a line range with sed
task: Display lines 3 through 6 from `app.log`.

title: Extract a configuration value
task: Print only the value assigned to `PORT`.

title: Save and display output with tee
task: Display severe log entries and save them to `workspace/severe.log`.

title: Use xargs to count lines
task: Count lines in every project Markdown file.

title: Use xargs with grep safely
task: Search every log file for ERROR using null-delimited paths.

title: Number every line
task: Display `paragraphs.txt` with line numbers.

title: Reverse line order
task: Display the application log from last line to first.

title: Reverse characters
task: Reverse the characters of each line in `names.txt`.

title: Verify duplicate contents
task: Calculate checksums for the suspected duplicate files.

title: Compare files byte by byte
task: Verify whether the two duplicate files are identical.

title: Show differences between reports
task: Compare January and February reports in unified format.

title: Inspect disk usage
task: Display the total apparent disk space used by each top-level practice directory.
cmd: du -sh data logs projects backups temp permissions mixed reports 2>/dev/null | sort -h

title: Build a top-IP pipeline
task: Print the three most active IP addresses and request counts.
cmd: cut -d' ' -f1 data/web_access.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head -n 3
